qa.question: What is the average forecast error (overestimate) for survey respondents?
Is there differential attrition in the survey by contract type?
qa.answer: No, p-value for contract type balance is 0.614

equations.label: eq:beta-delta
equations.description: Beta-delta quasi-hyperbolic utility: $\beta < 1$ captures present bias, $\delta$ governs long-run patience.

qa.question: What does beta < 1 capture in the beta-delta model?
qa.answer: Present bias: extra discounting of all future periods relative to the present

equations.label: eq:contract-choice
equations.latex:
V^M > V^{PPV} \iff \sum_{t=1}^{T} \delta^t  eta [b - c(
u_t)] - p^M > \sum_{t=1}^{T} \delta^t  eta [b - c(
u_t)] \mathbf{1}[
u_t > 0] - p^{PPV} 
u_t
equations.description: A naive agent chooses monthly contract if perceived net value $V^M$ exceeds pay-per-visit $V^{PPV}$, based on forecasted $\nu_t$ but evaluated at $\beta=1$.

equations.label: eq:attendance
equations.description: Period-$t$ attendance decision: member exercises if present-biased benefit net of stochastic effort cost $c_t$ exceeds the current-period price $p_t$.

equations.label: eq:overconfidence
equations.description: Overconfidence parameter $\theta$: $\theta=1$ is fully naive (believes $\beta=1$); $\theta=0$ is fully sophisticated.

equations.label: eq:structural-lik
equations.latex: \\mathcal{L}(\\beta, \\delta, \\theta, b, c, \\kappa) = \\prod_{i=1}^N P(k_i \\mid \\beta, \\delta, \\theta) \\cdot \\prod_{t} P(\\nu_{it} \\mid \\beta, c, p_t) \\cdot P(s_i \\mid \\theta, c)
equations.description: Full information MLE: joint likelihood over contract choices $k_i$, monthly visits $\nu_{it}$, and survey forecasts $s_i$.

qa.question: What three sources of moments identify the structural parameters?
qa.answer: Contract choices, monthly attendance, and survey forecast data

equations.label: eq:identification
equations.description: Beta is identified by the covariance between contract choice and subsequent attendance: naive agents systematically choose costly contracts and attend infrequently.

equations.label: eq:per-visit-cost
equations.description: Quadratic per-visit cost function: $c_0$ is fixed hassle cost, $c_1$ and $c_2$ capture increasing marginal effort.

equations.label: eq:welfare-cs
equations.latex: CS = E\\left[\\sum_{t=1}^{T} \\delta^t [b - c_t] \\mathbf{1}[\\nu_t^*=1] - p^M\\right] - E\\left[\\sum_{t=1}^{T} \\delta^t [b - c_t] \\mathbf{1}[\\tilde{\\nu}_t^*=1] - p^M\\right]
equations.description: Consumer surplus loss from present bias: difference between time-consistent ($\beta=1$) and biased ($\beta<1$) surplus.

equations.label: eq:hazard
equations.latex: h(t \mid x) = h_0(t) \exp(x'\gamma), \quad h_0(t) = p \lambda (\lambda t)^{p-1}, \quad \mathbb{P}(T > t) = \prod_{k=1}^{K} \exp\!\left(-\binom{t}{k} \lambda_k \right)
equations.description: Proportional hazard model for cancellation: $h_0(t)$ is the Weibull baseline hazard with shape $p$ and scale $\lambda$.

equations.label: eq:optimal-contract
equations.latex: \\max_{p^F, p^M} \\pi = N \\cdot [p^F + p^M E[\\nu^*]] \\quad \\text{s.t.} \\quad CS(\\beta, p^F, p^M) \\geq \\bar{u}
equations.description: Firm's optimal contract design: maximize profit from fixed fee $p^F$ and per-visit fee $p^M$ subject to consumer participation constraint.

qa.question: What is the main empirical finding about monthly contract choice?
qa.answer: Monthly contract holders attend only 4.17 times/month on average, below the 4.5-visit break-even, paying more than pay-per-visit
